title: CS Education in the Era of Vibe Coding

statements.text: Prompt design merits equal curricular weight as algorithm design in modern CS programs.
Requiring students to code unaided is becoming as outdated as handwritten numerical analysis.
Vibe coding weakens abstraction skills by rewarding outputs over mechanistic understanding.
Introductory CS should teach model critique before teaching syntax.
Graduates who cannot build without AI assistance are still professionally competent engineers.
Assessment should prioritize system specification and verification over code authorship.
Mandating manual debugging in coursework misrepresents contemporary software practice.
AI-assisted development makes deep knowledge of data structures less educationally central.
Banning generative AI in CS classrooms now undermines academic integrity rather than protecting it.
The core aim of CS education should shift from programming mastery to computational judgment.

context.topic: Computer Science education in the age of vibe coding.
context.additional_context: The poll is part of a Computer Science Seminar talk.
context.additional_instructions: Look from all different angles, both optimistic and pessimistic.
